title: Marx and Engels in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The statues survived because Berlin treats them as evidence, not instruction.** They show how the GDR placed itself in the historic centre.
**The monument opened in April 1986,** only three years before the Berlin Wall fell.
**Marx and Engels were thinkers, not GDR officials,** which made the post-1990 removal question more complicated than a dictator statue.
**Berlin removed some symbols and kept others.** The Lenin monument in Friedrichshain was dismantled, while Marx and Engels remained in public view.
**The 2025-2027 redesign keeps the statues in place,** even while the wider Rathaus- and Marx-Engels-Forum area changes around them.
